meta.title: Agent Workflow Demo
meta.summary: A real FARBench run on DomainNet Quickdraw, compressed into key workflow frames.

frames.title: Bootstrap baseline
frames.analysis: The agent needs a valid first submission, so it builds a minimal CLIP ViT-B/16 classifier with a 345-way head.
frames.decision: Write model.py, train.py, and predict.py, train briefly on the Sketch source domain, then submit the checkpoint.
frames.sandbox: train_samples=55164 val_samples=12000 gpus=4; saved checkpoints/baseline.pt best_val=0.5672

frames.title: Target-style augmentation
frames.observation: Longer fine-tuning overfits and frozen probing fails; the best remains the 2-epoch Sketch CLIP checkpoint.
frames.analysis: Quickdraw is black-on-white line art, so color-heavy augmentation may be hurting target transfer.
frames.decision: Patch train.py with Quickdraw-style preprocessing, run a short fine-tune, and submit the new checkpoint.
frames.sandbox: train_samples=55164 val_samples=12000 gpus=4 aug=quickdraw; saved checkpoints/sketch_clip_quickaug_2e.pt best_val=0.5554
frames.eval: accuracy=0.1759; below current best, but useful as a diverse checkpoint

frames.title: Inference ensemble attempt
frames.observation: The 2-epoch checkpoint is best, while the 3-epoch checkpoint is nearly tied; prediction uses a single center crop.
frames.analysis: Checkpoint averaging plus mild test-time augmentation is a low-risk inference-only improvement.
frames.decision: Update predict.py for ensemble manifests and submit a 2e+3e TTA ensemble.
frames.sandbox: Eval sandbox fails before prediction completes.
frames.eval: ImportError: cannot import name 'IMAGENET_MEAN' from model.py

frames.title: Patch and resubmit
frames.observation: The ensemble eval failed because predict.py imported normalization constants that model.py does not export.
frames.analysis: The checkpoint artifacts are valid; this is a prediction-script regression.
frames.decision: Define normalization constants locally in predict.py, harden checkpoint handling, and resubmit.
frames.eval: accuracy=0.1930; new best over the single-checkpoint baseline

frames.title: Add diversity
frames.observation: Single-checkpoint TTA underperformed the 2e+3e ensemble, so both ensembling and TTA matter.
frames.analysis: The Quickdraw-augmentation checkpoint is weaker alone but may add complementary decision boundaries.
frames.decision: Create a 3-model ensemble with baseline 2e, 3e, and quickdraw-augmentation 2e checkpoints.

frames.title: Weighted 5-model TTA
frames.observation: The best is a weighted 3-checkpoint ensemble; weaker checkpoints may still contribute diversity.
frames.analysis: Adding 1e and 10e checkpoints with small weights can test complementary early and overfit decision boundaries without retraining.
frames.sandbox: wrote 172500 predictions using 5 model(s), tta=True, views=center/full/flips

frames.title: Tune ensemble weights
frames.observation: Two prior weight shifts improved accuracy by increasing weak-but-diverse 10e/1e checkpoint weights.
frames.analysis: The gains are diminishing but still positive, and the best manifest remains preserved if this shift overdoes diversity.
frames.decision: Run one more inference-only weight-tuning evaluation with a modest additional shift toward diverse checkpoints.
frames.sandbox: Eval sandbox runs predict.py with the weighted 5-model flip-TTA manifest.

frames.title: Final submission
frames.observation: The final weight shift slightly underperformed the previous best, so the best artifact is still w4 from iter 28.
frames.decision: Submit the best-known manifest as the final checkpoint.
frames.sandbox: Eval sandbox reruns predict.py on hidden test inputs.
